exercises.title: Orient on a fresh box
exercises.prompt: You've just SSH'd into a fresh Ubuntu GPU instance. Without a GUI, find out where you are, what's in your home directory, how much disk is free, and how much memory the machine has — using four commands.
exercises.solution:
```bash
pwd            # where am I (usually /home/ubuntu)
ls -la ~       # what's in home, including dotfiles
df -h          # disk space per mount — watch /home and /
free -h        # RAM total/used/free
nvidia-smi     # bonus: confirm the GPU is actually attached
```

These four orient you instantly on any unfamiliar Linux box — the first thing to run before you start paying for GPU time downloading datasets that won't fit.

exercises.title: Fix 'Permission denied'
exercises.prompt: A shell script you uploaded won't run — `Permission denied`. And a data directory owned by `root` won't let you write to it. Diagnose and fix both with the right permission/ownership commands, and explain what the `rwx` bits mean.
exercises.hint: `ls -l` shows permissions/owner; `chmod +x` for execute; `chown -R` (with sudo) to take ownership.
exercises.solution:
```bash
ls -l run.sh                      # -rw-r--r-- : no execute bit
chmod +x run.sh                   # add execute
./run.sh                          # now runs

ls -ld /data                      # owned by root
sudo chown -R $USER:$USER /data   # take ownership recursively
```

Each file has three permission triples — **owner / group / others** — each with read(r), write(w), execute(x). `-rw-r--r--` means owner can read+write, everyone else read-only, nobody can execute. Scripts need the **x** bit; directories you write to must be **owned by you** (or group-writable). `chmod` changes permissions, `chown` changes ownership.

exercises.title: Provision a GPU box
exercises.prompt: Write the sequence of commands to take a bare Ubuntu instance to ready-for-AI: update packages, install build tools + git + a Python toolchain, clone a repo, and confirm the GPU is visible. Note the macOS habits that will trip you up here.
exercises.hint: `apt update && apt upgrade`, `apt install build-essential git`, install `uv`, `git clone`, `nvidia-smi`.
exercises.solution:
```bash
sudo apt update && sudo apt -y upgrade
sudo apt install -y build-essential git curl
curl -LsSf https://astral.sh/uv/install.sh | sh   # Python toolchain
git clone https://github.com/you/your-repo.git && cd your-repo
nvidia-smi                                        # GPU present?
```

**macOS → Linux gotchas:** there's no `brew` (use `apt`); paths are case-sensitive (`Data/` ≠ `data/`); the default shell may be bash, not zsh; `sudo` is required for system installs; and line endings matter — a script saved on Windows with CRLF will fail with a cryptic `bad interpreter` until you `dos2unix` it.
